start.speaker: narrator
start.text: The path ends at a clearing. Rivendale — or what remains of it. Stone foundations rise from the weeds like broken teeth. The mill wheel lies on its side, half-buried. But there is smoke. Thin and white, rising from somewhere among the ruins.

options.text: [Show banner] I carry the banner of the Silver Wolves! Is anyone here?
Approach quietly and observe before revealing yourself.
Check the edges of the village for graves first.

survivor_response.speaker: narrator
survivor_response.text: Silence. Then a stone scrapes. A trapdoor opens in the foundation of what was once the schoolhouse. An old woman peers out, eyes wide.

old_woman_speaks.speaker: rivendale_elder
old_woman_speaks.text: The Silver Wolves. After seven years. Is this a trick? Did the Ironmarch send you?

options.text: Commander Voss sent me. She wants to know if anyone survived.
I'm a traveler. I found this banner and followed the smoke.

elder_reacts_voss.speaker: rivendale_elder
elder_reacts_voss.text: Voss. The young lieutenant who wept when they ordered the retreat. I saw her face through the flames. She didn't want to leave us. I've always known that.

survivors_emerge.speaker: narrator
survivors_emerge.text: More faces appear. Six others — gaunt, weathered, but alive. Seven survivors of Rivendale, living in the root cellars beneath the ruins for seven years. Growing mushrooms. Catching rainwater. Hiding from every patrol that passes.

elder_message.speaker: rivendale_elder
elder_message.text: Tell Voss we don't blame her. We never did. The captain gave the order. She followed it. That's what soldiers do. But tell her this — we're still here. And we'd very much like to stop hiding.

elder_cautious.speaker: rivendale_elder
elder_cautious.text: A traveler with a military banner. Forgive my suspicion — the last strangers who came here wore Ironmarch steel.

observe_ruins.speaker: narrator
observe_ruins.text: From cover, you watch. The smoke rises from a hidden chimney — an underground dwelling built into the old foundations. No soldiers. No Ironmarch flags. Just... silence and survival.

options.text: Check the wider area first — is Ironmarch nearby?

ironmarch_check.speaker: narrator
ironmarch_check.text: To the east, fresh boot prints in the mud. Ironmarch patrol tracks, less than a day old. They've been circling the ruins. They know someone is here.

approach_now_urgent.speaker: narrator
approach_now_urgent.text: There isn't much time. The Ironmarch will return. Whatever you find here, you need to find it quickly.

find_graves.speaker: narrator
find_graves.text: At the edge of the village, beneath a stand of birch trees: thirty-two cairns. Each marked with a river stone, each stone carved with a name. Families. Children. A whole community reduced to neat rows of rock.

options.text: Read the names carved into the stones.
The smoke is still rising. Someone is alive here despite the graves.
Thirty-two graves. That's the answer Voss needs.

names_read.speaker: narrator
names_read.text: Aldrin Thatcher. Mira Thatcher. Young Aldrin. Bessa the Baker. Tomlin Greaves and his three daughters. Name after name. But the count stops at thirty-two. The village had thirty-nine residents. Seven are unaccounted for.

leave_graves.speaker: narrator
leave_graves.text: You take one of the smaller stones as proof. Voss will need something to hold. Something real. The walk back feels longer than the walk there.
